name: Slam Stewart
abstract: Leroy Eliot "Slam" Stewart (September 21, 1914 – December 10, 1987) was an American jazz double-bass player whose trademark style was his ability to bow the bass (arco) and simultaneously hum or sing an octave higher. He was a violinist before switching to bass at the age of 20.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I made some recordings with the Slam Stewart Quartet with John Collins on guitar, with Harold Doc West on bass, and Slam Stewart and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Slam a lot and got to play on Slam’s last album with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: Bucky came home from Marty’s with Slam and Bucky would go to bed and I would say, and there was Ovaltine and so Slam loved to sit there... I would sort of sit there and watch him have cookies and Ovaltine
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Hot Lips Page comes over to me and he says, “Hey man, Slam is in the dressing room and he’s not feeling good... Can you fill in for bass?” ... I got on the bandstand
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been overseas another time with Slam Stewart, Johnny Guarnieri, Pat Flowers and we did a tribute to Fats, it was called a “Tribute to Fats Waller.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I worked a few months with Benny, and the time I was with him, Slam Stewart was with him, and Red Norvo was with him same time I was with him.
